ideas.title: AD — Why are we still typing between sets?
ideas.concept: Voice logging vs. typing. The headliner ad — side-by-side friction vs. magic.
ideas.hook: Why are we still typing between sets?
ideas.body:
[0s] Finish a hard set, rack it. Pick up phone, unlock, tap through menus, fat-finger the weight -> visibly annoyed. EXAGGERATE the friction.
[hard cut] Same lifter, next set. Tap the mic: "225 for 5." Set logs instantly.
[end frame] "Say it. Log it. Build."

Length: 10-12s. The contrast IS the ad.
ideas.cta: Say it. Log it. Build — free on the App Store.

ideas.title: AD — Stop rebuilding workouts one exercise at a time
ideas.concept: Photo import. Snap any workout (screenshot/PDF/paper) -> instant routine.
ideas.hook: Stop rebuilding workouts one exercise at a time.
ideas.body:
Show a complicated workout: Instagram screenshot / Notes / a coach's PDF / paper.
Fast cut or split screen: LEFT = typing it in exercise by exercise (painful). RIGHT = Build, snap the photo -> full routine appears, exercises matched.
[end frame] "Snap it. Lift it. Build."

Length: 12-15s. Visibly does something other trackers can't.
ideas.cta: Snap it. Lift it. Build — free on the App Store.

ideas.title: AD — Tracking is finally easier than NOT tracking
ideas.concept: Fast montage of the whole loop: voice log -> see last week -> beat it -> history -> finish.
ideas.hook: POV: tracking your lifts is finally easier than not tracking them.
ideas.body:
Quick cuts: voice-log a set -> app shows last week's weight -> you beat it (PR flash) -> scroll the filling-up history -> finish-workout animation.
[end frame] "Build — free on the App Store."

Length: 12-15s.
ideas.cta: Build — free on the App Store. Link in bio.

ideas.title: AD — The mirror lies. The numbers don't.
ideas.concept: Progress charts. Sells the emotional payoff, not just a feature.
ideas.hook: Progress you can't see in the mirror.
ideas.body:
Two near-identical gym clips weeks apart (same shirt = funnier).
Reveal the chart: more weight, more reps, higher volume, new PR climbing.
[end frame] "The mirror lies. The numbers don't. Build."

Length: 12-15s. Strongest once you have real data to screen-record.
ideas.cta: The mirror lies. The numbers don't. Build — free.

ideas.title: AD — Stop doing barbell math
ideas.concept: Plate calculator. Universal pain, instant payoff, 10-min shoot.
ideas.hook: If you still count plates in your head, watch this.
ideas.body:
Squinting at a loaded bar, unsure how much is on it.
Cut to Build -> exact plates per side for your target weight. Load it, lift.
[end frame] "Build does the math. You do the lift."

Length: 7-10s.
ideas.cta: Build does the math. You do the lift. Free on the App Store.

ideas.title: AD — Walked in with no plan? Fixed in 5 seconds.
ideas.concept: Prebuilt programs (Explore). Aimed at beginners who freeze at 'what do I do today.'
ideas.hook: Walked in with no plan? Fixed in 5 seconds.
ideas.body:
Open Explore -> tap a prebuilt program -> it drops a folder of ready routines -> start day 1, log first set by voice.
[end frame] "~30 ready-made programs. Build."

Length: 8-12s.
ideas.cta: Build — free on the App Store. Link in bio.

ideas.title: UGC — Snapchat: bro's been talking to his watch all workout
ideas.concept: Your idea: someone films you on Snap captioned 'bros been talking to his watch the whole workout' — then reveal it's voice-logging on the Apple Watch.
ideas.hook: Caption over candid footage: "bro's been talking to his watch the entire workout 💀"
ideas.body:
[shot 1] Candid, filmed-from-across-the-gym vibe (have a friend hold it, or fake the angle). You between sets, talking to your wrist. Snap caption: "bros been talking to his watch the whole workout."
[shot 2] Cut to a close-up of YOUR view: tap the watch, say "incline, 75 for 8" -> the set logs on the wrist. Look up like nothing happened.
[text] "...he's just logging his sets 😭" / "it's Build."

Length: 10-15s. Feels like a real candid clip, not an ad. The watch angle = free Apple Watch promo too.
Variation: do the same joke with the PHONE propped on the bench instead of the watch.
ideas.cta: It's Build — free on the App Store. Link in bio.

ideas.title: UGC — You said you'd remember the weight. You didn't.
ideas.concept: Relatable comedy. Forgetting your numbers post-workout. Barely feels like an ad.
ideas.hook: Walking out of the gym, talking to camera: "solid session today."
ideas.body:
[setup] Post-workout glow, walking out: "solid session."
[turn] Freeze. Text slams up: "You said you'd remember the weight." Beat. Next: "You didn't."
[payoff] Quick cut to you mid-session tapping the mic -> "incline, 75 for 8" -> logged. "yeah I don't do that anymore."

Length: 10-12s. Caption: "every. single. time. 😭 (app is Build)"
ideas.cta: app is Build — free on the App Store.

ideas.title: UGC — Can it understand this? (TEST SERIES)
ideas.concept: Stress-test the voice logger with realistic phrases. Repeatable series — make Part 2, 3, 4.
ideas.hook: "Let's see if Build can actually keep up."
ideas.body:
Real gym, music playing. Fire phrases at the mic, show each one log:
- "225 for 5." OK
- "same weight, 4 reps." OK
- "bump it 10 pounds." OK
- "three sets of eight." OK
- say it out of breath / with gym music blasting OK

Length: 12-20s each. Caption: "ok I'm impressed 😅 what should I make it log next?"
Builds trust + invites comments (= reach) + endlessly repeatable.
ideas.cta: Build — free on the App Store. What should I test next?

ideas.title: UGC — Stealing my friend's workout (photo import)
ideas.concept: Frames photo import as a normal lifter behavior — copying a friend's plan from a screenshot.
ideas.hook: "My friend sent me his push day as a screenshot. Watch this."
ideas.body:
Hold up the messy screenshot.
In Build: snap it -> full routine builds itself, exercises matched.
"I'm literally just gonna do his workout now." Start lifting, voice-log the first set.

Length: 15-20s. Caption: "stealing everyone's workouts now 📸"
ideas.cta: Build — free on the App Store. Link in bio.

ideas.title: UGC — Real set, real log (raw, one take)
ideas.concept: Unpolished proof it works in an actual gym. No cuts, no editing magic.
ideas.hook: "this is the whole reason I switched trackers."
ideas.body:
ONE take, on purpose raw. Phone propped on a bench.
Finish the set, rack it, tap mic: "incline bench, 75 for 8." Watch it appear. Straight back to lifting.
No fancy captions, no cuts.

Length: 8-10s. Raw > slick for trust.
ideas.cta: Build — free on the App Store.

ideas.title: UGC — Gym tracker personalities (sketch)
ideas.concept: Comedy sketch: play each type of tracker. Build is the obvious winner without saying 'buy this.'
ideas.body:
Quick angle/costume changes, play each:
- The Notes-app lifter (chaos, typos)
- The "I'll remember it" guy (won't)
- The dude typing through his ENTIRE rest period
- The Build user: taps mic, "225 for 5," back to lifting in 2 seconds

Length: 15-25s. Caption: "which one are you 👀"
ideas.cta: be the last guy. Build — free on the App Store.

ideas.title: UGC — POV: you finally started tracking (aesthetic)
ideas.concept: Satisfying macro montage with trending audio. Pure vibes, zero hard sell.
ideas.body:
Sound-on, looping. Tight macro shots:
- tap to complete a set (the check + log-flash)
- rest ring counting down on the watch
- scrolling history
- the finish-workout animation
Trending audio over it.

Length: 10-15s.
ideas.cta: Build — free on the App Store.

ideas.title: UGC — Beating last week (the dopamine hit)
ideas.concept: Sells the come-back-tomorrow loop — last weight on screen is underrated retention.
ideas.hook: Walk up to the bar: "last week this crushed me."
ideas.body:
[setup] Walk up to the bar: "last week this crushed me." Show Build pulling up last session's number.
[payoff] Hit the set, log it, the weight/PR ticks up. "let's gooo."

Length: 8-10s. Caption: "the app showing me last week's number is lowkey why I keep going back 📈"
ideas.cta: Build — free on the App Store.
